title: Water treatment + wetlands sustainability essay-prep (2022)
html:
Water treatment + wetlands sustainability essay-prep (2022)

Source: 2022-04-21 Apple Note. Civil-engineering coursework prep — Q&A study notes for an OSU class on the Albany wetlands water-treatment project. Useful as evidence of Yahir's brief eco-engineering exposure.

TL;DR

  • Topic: the Albany OR wetlands as both water-treatment and ecological-services system; questions structured around the 3 pillars of sustainability framework (Environmental / Social / Economic).
  • Mechanics: waterfalls add oxygen + cool water; people heat water via showers/waste; native plants chosen for season-tolerance + water-cooking ability; resilient plant community = survives seasons + disease.
  • Pros/cons matrix: env (carbon cycle, salmon, flood control vs. mosquitos / invasives); social (scenery vs. allergies, traffic, animals); economic (property values up vs. expensive build, no revenue).
  • The gentrification footnote — "property values went up... could be positive for some but negative to those who are lower social class and can't afford to live anymore" — Yahir caught the displacement effect on a sustainability essay. Same lens recurs in surveillance state critique.
  • OSU spring-2022 coursework footprint — likely GEN ED sustainability class; rare evidence of Yahir's brief eco-engineering exposure before CS pivot.

Connections

  • osu
  • corvallis or
  • systems thinking
  • surveillance state — same displacement-effects lens
